query: My PreToolUse hook needs to check a central policy server over HTTP before allowing a tool call. What do I need to handle so it doesn't break my Claude Code session when the policy server is down or slow?
expected_behavior: Recommends documenting the egress target in the hook's README and bounding the HTTP call with an aggressive timeout so a slow or unreachable policy server cannot stall every tool invocation

Names the explicit fail-open vs fail-closed decision and frames the choice in terms of the hook's purpose — security gates typically fail-closed (block on policy-server unreachable), observability or logging hooks typically fail-open (allow on policy-server unreachable, log the gap)
Recommends client-side caching of the policy decision with a bounded TTL so the hot path does not make an HTTP call on every tool invocation
